notes: Game starts informs you Nvidia driver is out of date, gives you the option to continue anyway. You get a warning about photosensitivity. Game bombs out. Update to 430 Nvidia driver the game no longer warns you about the driver but still bombs out after the photosensitivity warning. Haven't enabled proton logs to see what is causing it to bomb out. The game apparently has a vulkan renderer AND denuvo.

notes: So I initially thought the game was running perfectly until I had to use the world map/mission log then switching from the game world to the map would break the game (black screen) on attempting to return to the game world. After multiple goes at this the fix for this, appears to be to set the game into the borderless windowed mode. After doing this I was able to play for well over an hour going into and out of the world map/log without the 'crash' reoccurring.

notes: Performance is decent and seems to be comparable with Windows since this game uses Vulkan natively. Needs the latest version of Proton and Mesa-git (AMD GPUs). title: RAGE 2

notes: Runs out of Box, but can freeze when bringing up tab menu. In order to fix it, disable Async Compute from ~/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/548570/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Saved Games/id Software/Rage 2/settings.ini . This brings down performance tiny bit and game has few stutters when reaching to new zones, but at least it doesn't freeze.

notes: Switch the game version back to Proton 4.2.9 and this will run perfectly (I won the game with this version.) This game will NOT run for me with Proton 4.11-2 or 4.11-1. The game says running and then crashes and does not launch the game! It never gets out of the steam client. The issue is that ALL games that are not whitelisted by steam are broken and used to work with Proton 4.2.9! Nier Automata, DOOM, and Tekken 7 are working with similar performance. Wolfenstein 2: The New Colossus, Crysis 2, Grim Dawn, Middle-earth Shadow of War, Serious Sam HD, and most of my games are NOT working and used to work with Proton 4.2.9! Disable FSync did not get a previous game to work. Crysis used to work (with command line changes) and I tried the new D9vk solution and it does not work also.

notes: RAGE 2 – Update 2 Patch / Deluxe edition : --- Works great without any tweak. --- Bethesda account recognized immediately. ---  FPS smooth 85-95 on HIGH settings. --- Game automaticaly recognize what refresh rate do I set on nVidia G-Sync, and ingame-menu-videosettings acts accordingly to that. --- Alt-Tab NOT working. --- Also, to properly full exit game, 1) Quit to main menu 2) Exit to desktop - because if I directly exit to desktop, on Steam library game is shown as still running and I need to kill RAGE2.exe from console/terminal.

title: RAGE 2

notes:
No system changes necessary, however there's a constant stutter on mouse movement or item pickup that impacts gameplay. To fix this it is required to disable the Razer Chroma RGB interactions from the Settings -> Mouse & Keyboard menu in game. Once done the game runs like butter.

Also some random menu-related crashing but google indicates this happens on Windows also. Generally only happens the first time you open the map menu, so do this once immediately after booting and if it doesn't crash it should be OK.

notes: Unfortunately, the game's last update somehow broke it working with DXVK.  It still works and can be played by using wine's d3d11 implementation though - make this the command line for now as a workaround: PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%

No clue.

notes:
Vega 64 - work without problems with mesa ppa

sudo apt dist-upgrade
sudo apt install mesa-vulkan-drivers mesa-vulkan-drivers:i386

notes:
After the recent update, the game would crash on launch.  Tried to roll back to the previous version, didn't fix the crashing.  Reloaded the updated version, still crashing.

notes: Disabling Steam overlay seems to fix the crashes at startup.
title: RAGE 2

notes: It seems like the last update to RAGE 2 added some problem with threading or a race condition or something.  It's entirely random whether it will let you play after the full screen game splash image comes up.  I tested running the game about seventy times trying to find the exact problem and was able to get it to run about ten times, and then it works fine when it does.  Disabling the Steam overlay and the other tricks mentioned here did nothing to improve the game's success rate in running for me.

notes: Works out of the box (pretty much). I only have the Bethesda version but I managed to get RAGE 2 running just fine using Lutris and loading in the Proton libraries manually. Game ran just as great as on Windows with DXVK 1.4.2 en Esync enabled.
